chapter_context.summary: Ahab allies with Jehoshaphat of Judah against Syria; ignoring the faithful prophet Micaiah, Ahab goes to battle in disguise and is killed by a random arrow; Jehoshaphat's reign in Judah is summarized.

outline.text: Ahab and Jehoshaphat Allied; False Prophets Promise Victory
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahab and Jehoshaphat Allied; False Prophets Promise Victory (22:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Micaiah Prophesies Ahab's Defeat and the Lying Spirit
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Micaiah Prophesies Ahab's Defeat and the Lying Spirit (22:13-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Battle at Ramoth-gilead; Ahab Disguises Himself and is Slain by a Random Arrow
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Battle at Ramoth-gilead; Ahab Disguises Himself and is Slain by a Random Arrow (22:29-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: King Jehoshaphat's Good Reign in Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Jehoshaphat's Good Reign in Judah (22:41-50), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: King Ahaziah's Wicked Reign in Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Ahaziah's Wicked Reign in Israel (22:51-53), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 22, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
